title: Before You Edit risk page must explain why and what to do first
summary: The viewer Risks page was not user comprehensible when it exposed raw labels like blast radius, risk signals, silos, and generated memory packet paths. It now presents a Before You Edit checklist: filter generated memory
body:
The viewer Risks page was not user-comprehensible when it exposed raw labels like blast radius, risk signals, silos, and generated memory packet paths. It now presents a Before You Edit checklist: filter generated memory paths out of user-facing risk rows, show real code paths, explain why each area is risky, and include a concrete 'Do first' safety step. A related git status parsing bug trimmed modified paths from mcp/... to cp/... because gitLines already stripped leading status whitespace; gitChangedFiles now uses parsePorcelainPath.
Evidence: User reported they did not understand the risks or what to do. Headless viewer smoke showed Before You Edit page title/heading, rows with 'Why' and 'Do first', mcp/... paths, no agent_memory packet noise, and no body overflow. npm test --prefix mcp passed 173 tests; git diff --check passed.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; git diff --check; local headless Chromium smoke against http://127.0.0.1:3128/
